# Facts and Figures

The facts and figures component displays statistical information in a visually compelling way. It supports various presentation styles including basic text, icon-only displays, and multiple font styles. The component is designed to highlight key metrics, statistics, and data points with optional icons and customizable theming.

## Interactive Preview

Use the controls panel below to experiment with the facts and figures properties in real-time. The component adapts to different presentation styles, alignments, and theme colors.

<Canvas of={FactsAndFiguresStories.Interactive} />
<Controls of={FactsAndFiguresStories.Interactive} />

## Presentation Styles

### Basic Style

The basic presentation style displays the statistic prominently with optional descriptive content below. This is the most common and versatile style.

**Use Case:** Display clear, readable statistics with explanatory text for context.

**Example:** "$52,000" with content "Annual grant for undergraduate students"

**Best For:**

- Financial figures with context
- Percentage statistics with descriptions
- Any metric requiring explanation

### Icon-Only Style

The icon-only style emphasizes visual representation with an icon instead of or alongside the statistic.

**Use Case:** Create visual impact with iconography representing the data point.

**Best For:**

- Visual storytelling
- When space is limited
- Creating visual patterns with multiple facts

## Font Styles

### Normal

Standard numeric display using the default font styling. Provides clean, modern number presentation.

**Use Case:** Most statistics and metrics, especially modern data and contemporary figures.

### Numeric Oldstyle

Traditional, classical number styling with varying heights and descenders. Provides an elegant, traditional appearance.

**Use Case:** Historical data, traditional institutional metrics, when matching classical design aesthetics.

## Alignment Options

### Left Alignment

Statistics and content aligned to the left. Standard reading flow, works well in most layouts.

**Use Case:** Traditional layouts, content-heavy presentations, when facts are in a vertical list.

### Center Alignment

Statistics and content centered. Creates visual emphasis and symmetry.

**Use Case:** Highlighted statistics, hero sections, when displaying a small number of key metrics.

## Component Theming

The facts and figures component supports theming through the color dial system, allowing color customization to match institutional branding or create visual hierarchies.

**Theme Options:** Multiple theme colors available through the CMS color dial configuration.

**Use Case:** Match department colors, create visual groupings, emphasize certain statistics.

## Technical Specifications

- **Atomic Level**: Molecule
- **Template**: `yds-facts-and-figures.twig`
- **Styles**: `_yds-facts-and-figures.scss`
- **JavaScript**: May include animation effects for stat display

### Accessibility

- **Semantic HTML**: Uses appropriate heading and list elements for structure
- **Icon Accessibility**: Icons include appropriate ARIA labels when used
- **Color Contrast**: All theme variations maintain WCAG AA contrast requirements
- **Screen Reader Support**: Statistics and descriptions are properly announced with context

### Behavior

- **Responsive Design**: Adapts layout for mobile, tablet, and desktop views
- **Icon Display**: Icons scale appropriately with text size
- **Theme Application**: Color themes apply consistently across all variations
- **Collection Support**: Multiple facts can be grouped in collections

## Collection Usage

Facts and figures are typically displayed in groups or collections. The component includes wrapper markup for collections:

```html
<ul
  class="facts-and-figures__group__wrap"
  data-facts-and-figures-collection-type="single"
>
  <!-- Individual fact items -->
</ul>
```

**Collection Types:**

- **Single**: Individual fact display
- **Group**: Multiple related facts in a collection

## Usage Guidelines

### Best Practices

- Use clear, concise statistics that are easy to understand at a glance
- Provide context with descriptive content when the statistic needs explanation
- Choose alignment based on surrounding layout (center for hero sections, left for content areas)
- Use icons purposefully to enhance understanding, not just for decoration
- Maintain consistent presentation style within a collection of facts
- Select appropriate font style based on the aesthetic context (modern vs. traditional)
- Use theming to create visual hierarchy or grouping of related statistics
- Keep statistic text short and scannable (large numbers, percentages, currency values)

### Things to Avoid

- Don't use overly complex or technical statistics without explanation
- Avoid mixing different presentation styles within the same visual grouping
- Don't use icons that are unclear or don't relate to the statistic
- Avoid very long descriptive content that overwhelms the statistic
- Don't sacrifice readability for visual style
- Avoid using too many different theme colors in close proximity
- Don't display statistics without proper source attribution when required

## Common Use Cases

### Institutional Metrics

**Example:** Enrollment numbers, graduation rates, research funding, faculty count

**Configuration:** Basic style, normal font, center alignment for emphasis

### Financial Information

**Example:** Endowment size, scholarship amounts, grant funding

**Configuration:** Basic style with content, appropriate currency formatting

### Achievement Statistics

**Example:** Rankings, percentages, award counts

**Configuration:** Can use icons for visual impact, center alignment for emphasis

### Historical Data

**Example:** Founding year, years of operation, historical milestones

**Configuration:** Numeric oldstyle font for classical aesthetic

## Implementation Notes

### Grouping Multiple Facts

When displaying multiple facts and figures together, wrap them in the appropriate collection container with `data-facts-and-figures-collection-type` attribute for proper styling and spacing.

### Icon Selection

Icons should be semantically related to the statistic they accompany. Common patterns:

- Dollar sign/money icons for financial figures
- People icons for enrollment/population statistics
- Award/trophy icons for achievements and rankings
- Book icons for academic metrics
- Globe icons for international statistics

### Number Formatting

Statistics should be formatted appropriately:

- Use commas for thousands separators (1,200 not 1200)
- Include currency symbols when appropriate ($52,000)
- Use + for "or more" values (1,200+)
- Use % symbol for percentages (95%)

## Related Components

- **Text**: For detailed textual information
- **Heading**: For section titles above fact collections
- **Icon**: For standalone icon usage
